contextBanner: [TBG | State Envelope / Capability Router v1 | harness/state-management | current main]
purpose: Introduce durable typed state objects and a compact current-state envelope. Lanes become consumers of materialized views over shared facts instead of carriers of mutable prose.
designRules: Facts, constraints, evidence, objectives, and authority become durable typed objects.
Skills and lanes become consumers of those objects, not carriers of state.
Lanes may annotate state but may not redefine shared facts.
Append-only stores for observations and evidence; supersede-or-invalidate for claims.
The envelope is a regenerated materialized snapshot, never hand-edited.
Each lane receives a filtered view of the envelope, not the entire world.

mergeSemantics.claim: Supersede or invalidate with a new object

allowedScope: create and validate state object schemas
create and update capability and constraint registries
create, merge, and materialize state envelopes
forbiddenScope: claim launcher, behavior, or live runtime proof from envelope validation
silently edit existing state objects instead of superseding them

requires: all state object schemas exist and parse
the capabilities registry references existing skill ids from the skill manifest
the invariant constraints registry covers the universal safety rules from AGENTS.md
the state envelope schema enforces typed object references
the state view schema enforces lane-scoped materialized views
Test-TbgStateEnvelope.ps1 passes under PowerShell Core and Windows PowerShell 5.1
